your_book:
Jaleel's book essentially flatlined on Monday, July 6 — a $29.81 unrealized gain on a $20,569.78 account (0.15%) masks a rough day under the hood. The damage was concentrated in three names: Rocket Lab (RKLB) cratered 7.34%, Lululemon (LULU) dropped 2.36%, and Danaher (DHR) fell 2.18%. RKLB is the most acute problem — it's already down 11.8% on cost, carries an AVOID verdict with a QARP of just 37.8, and today's move further stretches the gap between price and any credible thesis. LULU adds insult to injury: down 9.6% on cost, a live guidance-cut risk flag, and now shedding another 2.36% on the day. The one bright spot was ServiceNow (NOW), which added 1.51% and remains the book's largest dollar position at $3,022 with a +13.3% open gain. Samsara (IOT) popped 4.4% but also carries an AVOID verdict — that gain doesn't validate the hold.

The drift problem is sharpest in ADBE and BSX. Adobe is the largest position at $3,416 (16.6% weight) yet sits 11.1% in the red with a persistent narrative headwind around AI disruption — it slipped another 0.75% today. The QARP and STRONG BUY verdict argue the fundamentals haven't broken, but price and thesis have been diverging for long enough to demand honest scrutiny. Boston Scientific is down 6.8% on cost and carries an elevated risk flag after its FY26 guidance was slashed; it reports July 29. ZTS is also down 4.2% on cost despite a STRONG BUY rating and the book's highest QARP at 84.6 — worth watching but not yet drifting badly.

Cash is effectively zero at $2.49, so there's no dry powder to act on dislocations or cushion further drawdowns. The pharma cluster (GILD, NVS, NBIX, VRTX) was mostly quiet today, though NVS shed 2.76%. The drug-pricing policy overhang across that cluster remains live with a July 30 watch date. BSX earnings on July 29 and an unusual options sweep in KO ahead of its July 28 report are the near-term calendar items to keep on the radar.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated position down 7.34% today, −11.8% on cost — no thesis support
attention.note: RKLB carries the book's lowest QARP (37.8) and an explicit AVOID verdict; today's 7.34% single-day drop with no offsetting fundamental case makes the cost of continued holding concrete and rising.

attention.headline: Guidance-cut risk flag + down 9.6% on cost, another 2.36% today
attention.note: Americas comps are running −5% for a fifth straight period with tariff margin pressure and China stalled — the thesis is visibly deteriorating and the next earnings catalyst isn't until ~September 4.

attention.headline: Elevated risk flag; FY26 growth guide slashed, reports July 29
attention.note: BSX is already down 6.8% on cost following a guidance cut to 7–8.5% growth with management signaling flat performance through Q3 — the July 29 print could crystallize whether this is a temporary reset or a deeper problem.

attention.headline: Largest position at $3,416, down 11.1% on cost despite STRONG BUY — thesis/price gap widening
attention.note: Adobe beat and raised in June yet continues to sell off on AI-disruption narrative and CFO exit; at 16.6% of the book, the divergence between a high-conviction fundamental verdict and persistent price weakness warrants a genuine re-examination, not patience by default.
